etymology.description: semantic 米(米) (mǐ, "rice") + phonetic 㚇(㚇) (zōng) -> sticky rice wrapped in leaves
etymology.interpretation: The character 糉 originated as a phono-semantic compound where 米(米) (mǐ, "rice") indicates the semantic field relevant to rice, and 㚇(㚇) (zōng) provides the sound. Together they denote sticky rice wrapped in leaves, commonly referred to as 粽子(粽子) (zòngzi) in modern usage.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢在端午节吃粽子(我喜歡在端午節吃糉子)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān zài duānwǔjié chī zòngzi.
examples.english: I like to eat sticky rice wrapped in leaves during the Dragon Boat Festival.

examples.chinese: 她在市场上买了很多粽子(她在市場上買了很多糉子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài shìchǎng shàng mǎile hěn duō zòngzi.
examples.english: She bought a lot of sticky rice wrapped in leaves at the market.

examples.chinese: 粽子的味道让我想起了童年(糉子的味道讓我想起了童年)。
examples.pinyin: Zòngzi de wèidào ràng wǒ xiǎngqǐle tóngnián.
examples.english: The taste of sticky rice wrapped in leaves reminds me of my childhood.
